prose:
Method provenance  
Property: condensed Fukui functions (f+, f−, dual descriptor) for aniline; identification of the most electrophilic and nucleophilic atoms.  
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (chemkit tier ‘standard’), *no density-fitting* (exact four-center integrals).  
Geometry provenance: user-supplied coordinates from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz; no geometry optimisation – single-point SCF calculations for the neutral (N), cation (N–1) and anion (N+1) states were performed on this fixed structure (DFT//input-xyz).  
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).  
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (0,1); cation doublet (+1,2); anion doublet (-1,2).  
Software: PySCF 2.x via chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (engine subcommand `fukui`).  
Convergence & warnings: SCF cycles converged for all three charge states; integrity.trustworthy = true.  Warnings reported by the engine (verbatim):
